title: Efficacy of the Mindfulness Meditation Mobile App "Calm" to Reduce Stress Among College Students: Randomized Controlled Trial
headline: Efficacy of the Mindfulness Meditation Mobile App "Calm" to Reduce Stress Among College Students: Randomized C
summary: A randomized controlled trial of mindfulness / meditation examined sleep. Similar results were found for mindfulness and self-compassion. A significant group×time interaction in models of sleep disturbance was found, but no significant effects were found for other health behaviors.
keyFindings: Bonferroni posthoc tests showed significant within-group mean differences for perceived stress in the intervention group (P<.001), while there were no significant within-group mean differences in the control group (all P>.19).
Similar results were found for mindfulness and self-compassion.
A significant group×time interaction in models of sleep disturbance was found, but no significant effects were found for other health behaviors.
limitations: Single-trial result; requires replication, and self-reported outcomes may inflate apparent effects.
The practice studied (mindfulness / meditation) is not the Miracle of Mind practice; findings do not transfer directly to MoM.
